ticker.text: Masters R3 — McIlroy tee time 2:30 ET

nba.note: 15-game Friday · Hawks clinch playoff spot · PHI 105 IND 94 · BUK 125 NKS 108 · Regular season ends Sunday

nhl.note: Playoffs begin April 18 · Stanley Cup Playoffs Buzz: Sabres lead Atlantic · Avalanche top seed locked

wbc.label: MLB — Regular Season
wbc.note: Dodgers 9-3 · Yankees 8-2 · Brewers 8-3 · Ohtani on-base streak: 40 straight games

soccer.label: UCL — Quarter-Finals, 1st Leg
soccer.note: Second legs April 14–15 · Bayern lead Real Madrid · PSG control Liverpool tie

f1.result: Standings: Antonelli 72 pts · Russell 63 · Leclerc 49 — after 3 races (AUS/CHN/JPN)
f1.note: Antonelli won China + Japan · Next: Miami GP May 1–3

golf.result: Masters R2: McIlroy -12 · 6-shot lead · historic 36-hole margin at Augusta
golf.note: Reed & Burns T2 at -6 · R3 today on CBS · Final round Sunday

nascar.result: Food City 500 at Bristol today (12:30 ET · FOX) — Reddick leads Cup standings with 4 wins
nascar.note: Chase Elliott won Martinsville last time out · Bristol short track wildcard

topStories.headline: McIlroy at –12 with the largest 36-hole lead in Masters history — can anyone catch him?
body: Rory McIlroy birdied six of his final seven holes Friday to shoot 65 and reach 12-under par at Augusta National, taking a six-shot lead into the weekend — the largest 36-hole advantage in Masters history. He's seeking to become only the fourth player ever to defend the title at Augusta, joining Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo, and Tiger Woods.
Patrick Reed and Sam Burns are tied for second at –6. Bryson DeChambeau made a triple bogey at 18 and missed the cut. Round 3 is today on CBS with McIlroy's tee time at 2:30 ET.

topStories.headline: Bayern stuns Real Madrid, PSG silences Anfield — the quarterfinals have their stories
body: Bayern Munich beat Real Madrid 2–1 in the first leg on Tuesday, with Luis Díaz opening the scoring and Harry Kane doubling the lead within seconds of the second half. Real Madrid clawed one back but face a one-goal deficit heading to the Allianz Arena. On Wednesday, PSG dominated Liverpool 2–0 — Désiré Doué scored after 11 minutes and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia made it 2–0 in the 65th. The second legs are April 14–15.
Atlético Madrid beat 10-man Barcelona 2–0 with Julián Álvarez and Alexander Sorloth scoring. Arsenal edged Sporting CP 1–0 on a Kai Havertz goal in the 92nd minute.

topStories.headline: 40 points, 13 rebounds in 26 minutes — the fastest 40/10/5 game in NBA history
body: Victor Wembanyama reached 40 points, 13 rebounds, and 5 assists in just 26 minutes and 13 seconds on Friday, the fastest any player has ever recorded that stat line in NBA history. San Antonio won by 19 over Dallas. Wembanyama is now averaging 31.5 points, 12.4 rebounds, and 4.1 assists on the season — numbers that have the MVP conversation wide open with the playoffs one week away.

stories.headline: Celtics 144–118, Knicks clinch 3 — the East bracket is nearly locked
body: Boston dropped 144 points on New Orleans to clinch the No. 2 seed — Jaylen Brown led with 33 and Jayson Tatum added 28. The Knicks beat Toronto 112–95 to lock up the 3-seed, with Karl-Anthony Towns dominant at both ends. The Atlanta Hawks clinched a playoff berth with a win over Cleveland. The regular season ends Sunday — seedings will be finalized.

stories.headline: Wemby makes history, Nuggets beat OKC — 15-game Friday has everything
body: In a 15-game Friday slate with all 30 teams in action, Wembanyama's 40-point, 13-rebound, 5-assist performance in 26 minutes stole every highlight. Denver beat OKC 127–107 in a late-season statement. Philly beat Indiana 105–94 with Joel Embiid back healthy — the Sixers are playing confident basketball heading into the postseason.

stories.headline: Colorado clinches the Presidents' Trophy — MacKinnon's 52nd is a league record
body: The Colorado Avalanche won the Presidents' Trophy — awarded to the NHL's best regular-season record — by defeating Calgary 3–1 on Thursday. Nathan MacKinnon scored his 52nd goal of the season, the most by any NHL player this year, and his 110th point. Cale Makar added two assists and passed the 500-career-points mark earlier this week. Colorado enters the playoffs as the clear favorite.

stories.headline: Caufield reaches 50 — Canadiens are the Eastern Conference's hottest team
body: Cole Caufield scored his 50th goal of the season as Montreal edged Tampa Bay 2–1, with Juraj Slafkovsky netting the winner with 1:04 left in the third. The Canadiens have been one of hockey's best teams since February and are a dangerous playoff entry. Buffalo's Sabres are holding the Atlantic Division lead heading into the final weekend of the regular season.

stories.headline: Yankees and Dodgers out in front — Ohtani's on-base streak hits 40
body: Two weeks into the MLB season, the New York Yankees (8–2) and Los Angeles Dodgers (9–3) are the clear early movers. The Yankees have allowed just 8 runs in their first 9 games — tied for the fewest by any team in that span in modern history. Shohei Ohtani has reached base in 40 consecutive games, an MLB-best streak that stretches back to spring. The Brewers (8–3) are the NL's other early standout.

stories.headline: Bayern's Kane is unstoppable — Real Madrid's road to the semis just got harder
body: Harry Kane is having the season of his career at Bayern Munich, and it showed Tuesday. He scored within seconds of the second half to give Bayern a 2–1 lead that they protected to the final whistle against Real Madrid. Luis Díaz opened the scoring. Real Madrid head to the Allianz Arena April 15 needing to win — they've done it before, but Kane in this form is a different challenge.

stories.headline: PSG's depth is a problem for everyone — Liverpool need a miracle at home
body: PSG aren't just winning — they're winning convincingly. A 2–0 result at Anfield is an extraordinary result given Liverpool's home record. Désiré Doué (goal, 11') and Khvicha Kvaratskhelia (65') were clinical. Liverpool need to score three unanswered at the Parc des Princes April 14 to advance — possible in theory, unlikely given PSG's defensive discipline this season.

stories.headline: Antonelli 72, Russell 63 after three races — a teenage world champion is not impossible
body: Kimi Antonelli leads the Formula 1 World Championship with 72 points after winning the Chinese and Japanese Grands Prix. George Russell (63 pts) won Australia and has been his teammate's closest rival. Charles Leclerc sits third for Ferrari at 49 points. Mercedes are dominating the early season with their new 2026 regulation machinery. The next race is the Miami Grand Prix (May 1–3).

stories.headline: Monte Carlo Masters begins next week — the clay season opens with Sinner the heavy favorite
body: The ATP clay season officially opens with the Monte Carlo Masters starting April 13. Jannik Sinner arrives as the overwhelming favorite after his Sunshine Double sweep (Indian Wells + Miami without dropping a set). Carlos Alcaraz, a three-time Roland Garros champion, will be the main threat on clay. Novak Djokovic's clay-season schedule is still uncertain. The French Open is May 25 — Monte Carlo is the first major data point.

stories.headline: McIlroy is playing the round of his life — Augusta has never seen a lead like this
body: Through 36 holes, Rory McIlroy is playing the best Masters golf anyone has ever seen. His 65 on Friday — six birdies in a seven-hole stretch to finish — left him at 12-under, six clear of Patrick Reed and Sam Burns. It's the largest 36-hole lead in the tournament's 90-year history. McIlroy won his first Masters last year and is four rounds from becoming only the fourth player to successfully defend.

The cut line fell around even par. Bryson DeChambeau made triple bogey on 18 and is done for the week. Scottie Scheffler, world No. 1, is five back and needs a special round Saturday to get back into it. Round 3 is today on CBS.

stories.headline: Food City 500 today — can anyone slow Tyler Reddick down?
body: Tyler Reddick leads the NASCAR Cup Series standings with four wins in 2026, including a historic three-win streak to open the season. Chase Elliott won Martinsville two weeks ago to break Reddick's run. Today's Food City 500 at Bristol Motor Speedway (12:30 ET, FOX) is one of NASCAR's most unpredictable events — the concrete short track rewards aggression and punishes mistakes. Bristol has produced some of the wildest finishes in recent memory. Reddick goes in as the points leader, but Bristol plays by its own rules.
